ch03_intro.title: Fresh Air
ch03_intro.text:
The dark mood passes faster than you expect.

You breathe in the mountain air — clean and cold, nothing like the Outskirts. Back home, the air tastes of micro dust and filtration chemicals. Only the very rich breathe air like this. You breathe it now, chained to a line of strangers, climbing toward your death.

*'Truly, being chosen by the Spell has its benefits.'*

Ahead, a slave stumbles and does not get up. The soldiers drag him to the road's edge and toss him into the abyss. You watch him fall.

*'Poor fellow. Rest in peace.'*

All things considered, you are in good spirits.

ch03_decision_a.title: Strings of Fate
ch03_decision_a.text:
You were too outraged by your Aspect last time to read the Attribute descriptions carefully.

You have time now. The caravan drags itself up the mountain in silence. Your wrists ache, your feet are numb, and the cold is trying to peel the skin from your face. But Attributes represent one's natural traits and affinities — sometimes passive abilities. It might be worth knowing what you actually have.

*'Or I could just suffer in peace.'*

choices.label: Save your strength. You know enough already.

ch03_gameover_a.title: Blind in the Dark
ch03_gameover_a.text:
You decide you've seen enough. Temple Slave, three hidden Attributes — what's left to read?

Hours pass. The caravan stops. The soldiers light a bonfire.

Then the fire goes out.

And something comes down from the sky.

In the dark — mountain dark, real dark, with no city glow to soften it — you cannot see a thing. You don't know that shadows recognize you. You don't know your eyes work differently now. You run. Into a rock. Into the chain. Into the thing that is already there.

**[ YOU WERE NOT READY ]**

ch03_attributes.title: Your Attributes
ch03_attributes.text:
You focus inward. The runes sharpen.

**[ ATTRIBUTE: FATED ]**
*"The strings of fate wrap tightly around you. Unlikely events, both good and bad, are drawn by your presence. There are those who are blessed, and there are those who are cursed… but rarely both."*

**[ ATTRIBUTE: MARK OF DIVINITY ]**
*"You bear a faint scent of divinity, as though someone briefly touched by it once, a long time ago."*

**[ ATTRIBUTE: CHILD OF SHADOWS ]**
*"Shadows recognize you as one of their own."*

You stare at the last one for a long moment.

*'Hmmm. Interesting.'*

ch03_analysis.title: The Main Culprit
ch03_analysis.text:
[Fated] — that's the one responsible for all of this. It sounds like destiny, but the description is more honest: improbable things happen near you. Good ones and bad ones both.

*'This is how I ended up with a rare, useless Aspect. And a strange variant of it, at that.'*

[Mark of Divinity] allows passage into sacred places in the Dream Realm and enhances certain sorceries. Useful — if there were sacred places nearby, and if your Aspect had anything to do with sorcery.

[Child of Shadows] is the strange one. You have never heard of it. No obvious use comes to mind.

Not yet.

choices.label: Wait for the answer to come to you.

ch03_nightfall.title: Something Good
ch03_nightfall.text:
The sun hides behind the mountain. The sky turns from pale grey to black in minutes.

You notice you can still see.

Not faintly — not as shapes or outlines. Perfectly. The grain of the rocks, the expression on the nearest soldier's face, the frost on the other slaves' eyelashes. The treeline far below, clear as midday.

*'Child of Shadows. So that's what it does.'*

You look at the world with new eyes. The chains. The road. The dark edge of the abyss.

You file it away carefully.

*'Finally something good.'*

choices.label: The caravan slows. It will stop soon.

ch03_camp.title: Camp
ch03_camp.text:
*"Stop the caravan! Prepare to camp!"*

The slaves fall where they stand the moment the soldiers allow it. A clearing, partly sheltered by an overhang of rock. The soldiers attend to their horses before anyone lights a fire. The heavy wagon is pushed forward as a windbreak.

The stronger slaves crowd toward the bonfire. You end up at the outer ring, back to the cold, watching their warmth from a distance.

A soldier moves through with water and bread. A few sips. A piece of something rock-hard and moldy. You eat every crumb. You are still hungry.

ch03_decision_b.title: Bloodbane
ch03_decision_b.text:
A few steps past the end of the paving stones, bright red berries cluster in the snow. You noticed them along the road earlier — small, vivid things, almost cheerful against the white.

The slave chained behind you has noticed them too. His eyes glitter.

You are very hungry. The bread barely counted. The berries are right there.

*'How poisonous could mountain berries actually be?'*

choices.label: Resist. Wait and see what the others do.
Eat a handful. You need the energy.

ch03_gameover_b.title: Bloodbane
ch03_gameover_b.text:
The berries are tart, a little bitter. Not unpleasant.

Thirty minutes later, your stomach is on fire.

An hour later, you cannot feel your hands.

The man chained behind you, who nearly did the same thing, watches you convulse and says nothing. Somewhere up the mountain, something large is beginning its descent.

You don't live to see it arrive.

**[ CAUSE OF DEATH: BLOODBANE POISONING ]**

ch03_scholar.title: The Scholar
ch03_scholar.text:
The slave behind you lurches toward the berries on all fours.

*"I would advise against eating those, friend."*

The voice is calm and measured and completely out of place on this mountain. You turn.

A tall man in his forties. Lean, strangely handsome, with a dignity that does not belong on a slave chain. He might be a scholar — or he was, once.

*"Those are called Bloodbane. They grow in places where human blood was spilled. That is why there are always so many along the slave trade routes."*

*"So what?!"* the shifty slave behind you snaps.

*"Bloodbane is poisonous. A few berries might kill an adult man."*

The shifty slave flinches back. You say nothing. You were already not going to touch them.

ch03_vision_recog.title: The Death Site
ch03_vision_recog.text:
You have been looking at this camp without letting yourself think it.

Now you think it.

This is the place.

The bones from your vision at the start of the Nightmare — scattered across the road, buried under snow — they were here. This clearing. This overhang. This same bonfire.

The thought arrives without panic:

*'The attack will not be in days.'*

Above you, the mountain is very quiet. The soldiers have stopped talking. The fire crackles. Frost settles on your collar.

As if to answer your thoughts, a thundering noise rings from above.

ch03_end.title: Something Massive
ch03_end.text:
Something crashes down from the sky.

The impact hits before the sound does — a concussion that rattles your teeth, sends snow exploding outward in every direction. The horses scream. The bonfire lurches sideways. Chains snap taut.

You can see it perfectly in the dark.

Everyone else cannot.

**[ CHAPTER 3 COMPLETE ]**

*To be continued in Chapter 4: Mountain King*
